message:
fix(mux/client): use ts_milliseconds for ClientInfo timestamps (ft-ztcsl)

## Defect

frankenterm/mux/src/client.rs ClientInfo serialized its two
DateTime<Utc> fields — connected_at and last_input — via
chrono::serde::ts_seconds, which encodes the timestamp as
whole seconds since epoch. A JSON roundtrip therefore
truncated the sub-second portion:

  let t1 = Utc::now();              // e.g. 2026-04-22 14:02:03.123456
  let json = serde_json::to_string(&info);
  let back: ClientInfo = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  back.connected_at == 2026-04-22 14:02:03.000000  // .123456 lost

Two ClientInfo values that connected within the same
wall-clock second became JSON-equal — confusing every
"most-recent client" or "client count over last N seconds"
query that relied on the post-roundtrip ordering.

## Fix

Switch both fields to chrono::serde::ts_milliseconds. Same
integer wire shape (i64), 1000× the resolution. Millisecond
precision is enough to distinguish every plausibly-distinct
ClientInfo event — `Utc::now()` on every supported platform
advances at least 1 ms between successive calls in practice.

Scope check — ClientInfo is internal mux state. grep confirmed
no external JSON fixtures or wire-format tests pin the old
whole-second format, so the magnitude change (values are now
1000× larger) is safe.

## Regression test

`client_info_json_roundtrip_preserves_subsecond_ft_ztcsl`:
  - Construct a ClientInfo whose timestamps carry 123 ms of
    sub-second remainder.
  - Round-trip through serde_json::to_string +
    serde_json::from_str.
  - Assert connected_at and last_input equal the original
    (pre-fix ts_seconds would lose the .123 entirely).
  - Additional explicit check:
    back.connected_at.timestamp_subsec_millis() == 123.
    Prevents a future regress that flips back to ts_seconds
    from passing the whole-second equality comparison by
    coincidence on a timestamp whose ms happens to be 0.

Verified via:
  cargo test -p mux --lib \
    client::tests::client_info_json_roundtrip_preserves_subsecond_ft_ztcsl
→ ok (1m16s build including dependency graph, test itself 0.00s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
